evals.prompt: From the sourceweave-web-search repo, confirm that artifacts/sourceweave_web_search.py is still in sync with src/sourceweave_web_search/tool.py, then use the repo's standalone package workflow to run a quick 'python programming' smoke test that batch-reads the first two pages and includes search metadata.
evals.expected_output: Uses the current repo-local package workflow, validates artifact sync with sourceweave-build-openwebui --check, runs sourceweave-search with batched read_pages behavior, and reports results using the host-side 127.0.0.1 defaults rather than incorrectly calling container-name endpoints the package defaults.
expectations: Runs `uv run sourceweave-build-openwebui --check` before or as part of the verification flow.
Uses the package-level `sourceweave-search` CLI workflow with `--query`, `--read-first-pages 2`, and `--include-metadata` for the main smoke test.
Reports package behavior using the host-side defaults (`127.0.0.1` endpoints) instead of describing container-name endpoints as package defaults.
Checks that batched page reads and returned search metadata are part of the observed behavior, not just a plain search-only run.

evals.prompt: Check the deployment-aligned compose path without overriding any service URLs. Use the local docker stack's mcp service and tell me whether the runtime defaults still look correct there, while clearly separating compose container defaults from the package's normal host-side defaults.
evals.expected_output: Uses docker compose up -d mcp plus a compose-based sourceweave-search run, confirms the mcp service injects searxng/crawl4ai/redis container-network defaults, and reports any remaining risks without confusing those values with the package defaults.
expectations: Uses the compose `mcp` service for the deployment-aligned check instead of reusing package-level host assumptions.
Identifies `searxng`, `crawl4ai`, and `redis` container-network endpoints as the compose `mcp` defaults.
Explicitly distinguishes compose container defaults from the package's normal host-side defaults in the final report.

evals.prompt: Test the current direct read_pages behavior, not just the old search-then-single-read flow: read one HTML URL directly, then try a document URL and confirm it only succeeds when convert_document is set.
evals.expected_output: Exercises the current direct URL read path, distinguishes normal HTML reads from document-conversion reads, and confirms document URLs require convert_document instead of being treated as ordinary HTML reads.
expectations: Uses the explicit direct-read path (`--read-url` or equivalent) for both the HTML and document checks.
Shows that a normal HTML URL can be read directly without `convert_document`.
Confirms that a document URL without `convert_document` errors and that the same URL succeeds only when `convert_document` is set to true.
